title: 10 best herbal decoctions for colds
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for colds are a natural way to alleviate symptoms associated with upper respiratory infections. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their medicinal properties and antioxidants. By drinking these herbal teas, individuals can help soothe a sore throat, relieve congestion, and reduce fever. For example, peppermint and eucalyptus decoctions can ease coughs and open up airways, while ginger and lemon balm decoctions can provide anti-inflammatory relief. As a result, people can find natural relief from cold symptoms, improving their overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with colds because they contain compounds like phenolic acids, terpenoids, and flavonoids that have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to reduce congestion and relieve sinus pressure by soothing the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract. Additionally, purple coneflower's antiviral properties help to combat the underlying viral infection, reducing the severity of symptoms and shortening the duration of illness. By promoting a healthy immune response, herbal decoctions can provide natural relief from cold-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, sinuses, and lungs associated with cold symptoms.

Antiviral properties: The decoction's antiviral compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the replication of viruses responsible for common colds, reducing the severity and duration of infection.
Mucolytic activity: Purple coneflower's mucolytic properties help break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion, coughing, and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: IgE-inhibiting polysaccharides: These compounds help with colds by inhibiting the production of IgE antibodies, which contribute to allergic reactions and inflammation that often accompany colds.

Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating cold symptoms such as congestion and sore throat.

Alkylamides: These terpenes have immunomodulatory effects, which help stimulate the immune system to fight off invading pathogens and reduce the severity of cold symptoms such as coughing and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of echinacoside and alkylamides, which contribute to Echinacea's medicinal properties.
Leaves: They contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
Flowers: They are rich in polysaccharides and glycoproteins, which stimulate the immune system and support the body's natural defenses against infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers and leaves of the medicinal plant from mature stems in late summer.
Rinse the harvested plant material with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one to two teaspoons of the plant material with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters decoctions helps with colds because its unique blend of herbs, such as goldenseal and echinacea, stimulates the immune system to fight off infection. The decoction's antimicrobial properties also help to reduce congestion and inflammation in the throat and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms like coughing and sneezing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory effects can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce fever. By promoting a healthy immune response and reducing symptoms, King of bitters decoctions provides effective relief from cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: The decoction helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, which can alleviate congestion and coughing associated with colds.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal formula reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, which can help relieve symptoms such as sinus pressure, headaches, and sore throats.
Antiviral properties: The decoction contains herbs that have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses, including those that cause colds, which can help reduce the duration and severity of illness.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: A sesquiterpene lactone that helps with colds by inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Dihydrodehydroandrographolide: A sesquiterpene lactone that exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of colds such as cough, sore throat, and congestion.

6-Hydroxy-3-(4'-hydroxy-3'-methoxyphenyl)-8-premyl-8-azaspiro[4,5]deca-1,6,8-trien-2-one (Andropanolide): A sesquiterpene lactone that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory tract during colds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a compound believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Utilized for their andrographolide content, which may help alleviate cold symptoms.
Stems: Employed for their andrographolide and other bioactive compounds, which may aid in reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Rinse the dried herb with 100ml of water to remove any impurities or debris.
Steep 5g of the dried herb in 100ml of boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before consuming 20ml as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with colds because they contain potent bioactive compounds such as triterpenoid saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. These compounds work to soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and alleviate respiratory issues associated with the common cold. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antiviral activity helps to combat viral infections that cause cold symptoms, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas coneflower decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of a cold.
Antiviral properties: The decoction's antiviral properties help combat viral infections that cause colds, inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Immune system support: Kansas coneflower contains immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the body's natural defenses, boosting the immune system's response to infection and helping to prevent complications from a cold.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms to fight off infections and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
Triterpene glycosides: Specifically, compounds like 11-hydroxy-11, 18-dihydroechinacin have shown anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with colds.

Cichoric acid: This caffeic acid derivative has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development and severity of colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used to make decoctions due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside and caftaric acid, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions that can be beneficial in treating colds due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are used to make decoctions that can aid in treating colds due to their content of phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia and store them in airtight containers.
Combine the dried roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes or up to 30 minutes for a stronger decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Drink the decoction 2-3 times a day, 1-2 cups at a time, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with colds because it contains compounds that have potent antiviral properties. The decoction's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce the severity of cold symptoms by inhibiting the replication of viruses within the body. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus and promote its clearance from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing. By targeting multiple aspects of the cold, maidenhair tree decoctions can provide effective relief and support during the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help combat free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation associated with colds.
Mucolytic properties: Ginkgo biloba's mucolytic compounds break down excess mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion symptoms like coughing and wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion, sinus pressure, and headache pain associated with colds.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing the severity of cold symptoms by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress.

Bilobalide: This sesquiterpenoid has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help in alleviating symptoms of colds such as headaches and sore throats by reducing inflammation and promoting neural recovery.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the severity of cold symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to cold-related inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which helps to relieve respiratory issues associated with colds.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used due to their rich content of ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate cold symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Ginkgo biloba contain a higher concentration of ginsenosides, which can aid in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function during a cold.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late summer or early fall.
Dry the leaves in a warm place at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 weeks.
Chop 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes then strain it before drinking the decoction.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make decoctions for colds because they contain high levels of antiviral and antimicrobial compounds that help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Sambucus nigra are used to make decoctions for colds because they have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a trusted source in the morning.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a warm oven at 150.
Measure out 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves and add it.
Simmer the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes over low heat to allow the active.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme decoctions helps with colds because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and decongestant properties. The steam from the decoction can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in creeping thyme can help combat the underlying infection, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and fatigue. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a faster recovery when used consistently and in combination with other holistic treatments.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Creeping thyme's decoction has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat the viral infection responsible for colds and reduce its severity.

Decongestant properties: Thyme's essential oils have a natural decongestant effect, helping to relieve nasal congestion, sinus pressure, and cough associated with colds by opening up airways and reducing mucus production.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol, another phenolic compound found in Thymus serpyllum, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate congestion and soothe a sore throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic acid, has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the severity of cold symptoms and prolong recovery time.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make decoctions due to their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help relieve congestion and coughs associated with colds.

Roots: Used to make decoctions due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves for decoction.
Combine the harvested herb with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or 5-10 minutes in a tea infuser.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pale coneflower decoctions helps with colds because it has been traditionally used to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and reduce fever. Additionally, pale coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate sinus pressure and relieve chest tightness. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help modulate the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells, which aids in fighting off viral infections that cause the common cold.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Echinacea pallida decoctions exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms by protecting cells from oxidative damage and inflammation.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides are known to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the activity of natural killer cells and increasing the production of cytokines, which are essential for fighting off viral infections and reducing the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which are believed to stimulate the immune system.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea pallida roots in the fall or spring when the plant is dormant to ensure quality.
Clean and dry the roots thoroughly to prevent contamination and preserve their potency.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of chopped echinacea pallida roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes before straining it to create a decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with colds because it contains berberine, a natural compound that exhibits antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. Berberine has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause colds, reducing their ability to spread and multiply in the body. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate congestion and reduce sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold. By drinking goldenseal decoctions regularly, individuals may experience relief from cold symptoms and support their immune system's natural defenses.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial activity: Golden seal's berberine compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria, helping to combat infection and reduce symptoms of a cold.
Mucolytic properties: Golden seal's mucilage content can help break down mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and coughing associated with colds.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Golden seal's alkaloids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, nasal passages, and sinuses that often accompany a cold.
remedy_constituents: Volatile Oils: These terpene-based compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce congestion and ease cold symptoms.
Berberine: As a phenolic alkaloid, berberine has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat cold-causing pathogens and reduce the severity and duration of colds.
Hydrastine: This phenolic alkaloid has astringent and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce mucus production, ease congestion, and prevent the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The root is the most commonly used part, as it contains the highest concentration of berberine, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate cold symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in decoctions, as they contain berberine and other alkaloids that provide relief from cold and flu symptoms, including congestion and cough.
Stems: The stems of Hydrastis canadensis are sometimes used in decoctions for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of dried root from mature plants in the fall season for optimal potency.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root powder with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 30-45 minutes or 3-5 minutes per ounce of root.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with colds because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, alleviating symptoms of a cough and congestion. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and recover from the cold.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties: Licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and allergic reactions in the throat and respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms such as sore throats and congestion.
Expectorant and mucolytic properties: The saponins present in licorice root help break down mucus and promote its clearance from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.

Immune-boosting and antiviral properties: Licorice root contains compounds like triterpenoids that have been shown to enhance immune function and exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including those that cause colds, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce congestion and alleviate coughs associated with colds.

Flavonoids (isoorientin and isovitaminin): These phenolic compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the underlying oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral infections, thus aiding in the recovery from colds.
Liquiritin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus, reducing congestion and coughs associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used to make decoctions for colds due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Stems are used in decoctions for colds as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Barks: Barks are used to make decoctions for colds as they contain phenolic compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat infection and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush and then rinse with cold water.
Cut the roots into smaller pieces and then weigh out 25g for every 250ml of water.
Combine the cut roots with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil then reduce heat to low.
Simmer for 10 to 30 minutes then strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with colds because of its natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the chest and nasal passages. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat, making it easier to breathe and speak. remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 2 cups of the fresh above-ground parts of taraxacum officinale in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested taraxacum officinale thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned taraxacum officinale into small pieces and combine them with 4 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry decoctions helps with colds because of its impressive antiviral properties, which inhibit the replication of viruses responsible for common colds. The anthocyanins present in elderberries have been shown to bind to and inhibit viral surface proteins, preventing them from entering host cells. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried elder flowers and leaves from a trusted source or store-bought package containing 100g of sambucus canadensis.
Combine the dried herb material with 500ml of boiling water in a large glass jar or pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbal compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container discarding the solids.
Store the final product in an airtight container and consume 2-3 times daily as needed for up to 7 days taking 1/2 cup of warm liquid three hours before meals.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers measuring 2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Boil filtered water in a pot for 10 minutes before preparing the infusion.
Add the dried herb mixture to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container after steeping.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers in amounts of one to two teaspoons per cup.
Combine the gathered herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant container to create a steeping mixture.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an airtight container for use as needed throughout the day.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried allium cepa flowers or two medium onions chopped into small pieces.
Combine the measured ingredients with one quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool completely before refrigerating it for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with colds because its soothing properties ease congestion and coughing. The plant's mucilages, rich in saponins and flavonoids, form a protective film in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. As the decoction is ingested, its expectorant properties help loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel. This natural remedy also possesses anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate sinus pressure and ease breathing difficulties, providing relief from cold symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Mullein contains compounds that inhibit the replication of viral particles, which can help to shorten the duration and severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, mullein roots help soothe sore throats and reduce coughing associated with colds.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Rinse the harvested plant material thoroughly with clean water and remove any debris.
Combine the plant material with 500ml of boiling water in a large pot and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container and discard solids.
Store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint leaves in bulk.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or two tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves per cup water.
Combine the measured ingredients with boiling water and let steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days and consume as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with colds because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves or 3 teaspoons of fresh rosemary leaves.
Combine measured rosemary with 1 quart of boiling water in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes and then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve.
Transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Take 1/4 cup of the cooled decoction as needed every few hours or before bed for relief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried pelargonium sidoides root or leaves in the morning.
Boil two cups of water in a pot for five minutes before adding the herbs.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to colds that are treated with herbal decoctions are bronchitis, coughs, and upper respiratory tract infections. Herbal decoctions can help soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and ease coughing. Decoctions made from herbs such as thyme, ginger, and slippery elm have anti-inflammatory properties, while those containing peppermint and eucalyptus may help clear mucus and relieve chest congestion. These herbal remedies can be used to create teas, tinctures, or syrups for effective treatment of these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with colds is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Eucalyptus. Echinacea is known for its immune-boosting properties, while Ginger helps to alleviate congestion and soothe a sore throat. Eucalyptus adds its decongestant properties, easing breathing and relieving sinus pressure. Drinking a warm decoction of these herbs can help to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and alleviating discomfort. This combination can be consumed as a tea or added to a warm bath for added benefits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Minerva Pediatrics, herbal decoctions can be beneficial in treating common colds due to the presence of various active compounds such as polyphenols, flavonoids, saponins, glucosides, and alkaloids, which are found in these herbal agents. The study highlighted the widespread use of non-pharmacological remedies, including herbal medicines, to relieve the common cold, indicating a potential therapeutic effect. However, the study also emphasized the need for further rigorous studies to confirm the efficacy and safety of these natural products in relieving the common cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions helps with colds because of its potent immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives that stimulate the production of white blood cells, enhancing the body's ability to fight off infections. It also reduces the severity and duration of cold symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea decoctions may help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion, providing relief from cold-related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: Acts as an anti-inflammatory, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: Have immunomodulatory properties, helping to regulate the immune system's response to a cold.
Polysaccharides: Stimulate the activity of immune cells, such as macrophages and natural killer cells, to fight off cold-causing pathogens.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are considered the most potent part of Echinacea purpurea due to their high concentration of alkaloids.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in decoctions for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their immunomodulatory effects and to provide a soothing, calming effect on the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 ounces of dried echinacea purpurea root and flowers from a trusted herbal supplier.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of the dried echinacea per 8 ounces of water for a decoction.
Combine the measured echinacea with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes to release the herbal properties and compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra decoctions helps with colds because of its natural antiviral properties, which inhibit the replication of the rhinovirus and other viruses that cause respiratory tract infections. remedy_recipe: Collect 30-60 grams of dried sambucus nigra berries from a trusted supplier or forage responsibly.

Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of the powdered sambucus nigra per 250ml of boiling water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris decoctions helps with colds because they possess antimicrobial properties that combat the underlying bacterial and viral infections causing the illness. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to have decongestant and expectorant effects, making it easier to breathe and cough up mucus. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat, providing relief from cold symptoms. This makes thymus vulgaris decoctions a popular natural remedy for colds and respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps with colds by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of viral and bacterial infections.
Carvacrol: Helps with colds by possessing antiseptic and expectorant properties, which can aid in relieving congestion and coughs.
Rosmarinol: Helps with colds by displaying antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by viral infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: They are used as an alternative to leaves, offering similar benefits and a more delicate flavor.
Stems: They can also be used to make decoctions, providing a slightly different flavor profile and still retaining some of the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers and store in a clean glass container.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan over high heat until boiling point is reached quickly.
Add the dried thymus vulgaris to the boiling water and reduce heat to low for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the thymus vulgaris decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis decoctions helps with colds because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats. The decoctions also have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the underlying infection causing the cold. Additionally, rosmarinic acid has natural expectorant properties, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm, providing relief and promoting recovery from colds.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps combat colds by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogens responsible for respiratory infections.
Carnosol: Contributes to reducing inflammation and fighting oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with colds, such as congestion and coughing.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which enable it to combat viral infections and boost the immune system, helping to alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help to relieve congestion and soothe respiratory issues.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and essential oils that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections.
Stems: They are a good source of rosmarinic acid, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers from the plant by cutting them at dawn when moisture levels are highest.
Dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in a single layer for at least 30 days at room temperature.
Measure out 10 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers for a decoction and combine them with 1 liter of water.
Boil the water and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 10 to 15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia decoctions helps with colds because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off invading pathogens. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving symptoms such as congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, its antiviral properties help to combat the underlying viral causes of the common cold, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the duration of illness. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to boost immunity and alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help by inhibiting the replication of rhinoviruses, which cause the common cold, and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea angustifolia decoctions help by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, and by modulating the immune system to fight off infections.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia decoctions help by stimulating the immune system, specifically by increasing the production of white blood cells, and by reducing the severity of cold symptoms such as fever and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are considered the primary medicinal part of Echinacea angustifolia due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
Leaves: They are used in decoctions to provide a milder alternative to the roots, while still offering some health benefits.
Flowers: They are used in decoctions to add a soothing and gentle effect to the preparation, often used in combination with other parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 30 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and measure out 1 teaspoon per 8 ounces.
Combine the chopped roots with 8 ounces of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes then strain it with a cheesecloth.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica decoctions helps with colds because they have potent decongestant properties that relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The active compounds, such as ephedrine and isoflavones, also have expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus and phlegm. Additionally, Ephedra sinica has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe a sore throat and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from cold symptoms, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine alkaloids: These help relieve congestion and cough symptoms by acting as a decongestant and expectorant, making it easier to breathe.
Sinic acid (a phenolic acid): This constituent helps reduce inflammation and alleviate fever associated with colds by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Ephedrine glycosides (e.g., ephedrine and pseudoephedrine): These help combat cold and flu symptoms by acting as a natural decongestant and bronchodilator, improving respiratory function.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of ephedrine, which helps to relieve respiratory congestion and reduce fever.
Leaves: They are used because of their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to aid in cold recovery.
Barks: They are used for their high alkaloid content, including ephedrine, which helps to ease respiratory symptoms and reduce the severity of colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica roots and stems in the early morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested ephedra sinica roots and stems in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Crush 9-15 grams of dried ephedra sinica roots and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed ephedra sinica powder with 300-500 ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the decoction for 15-30 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus decoctions helps with colds because they contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoctions' high mucilage content helps to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract linings, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, Verbascum thapsus contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to boost the body's natural defenses and combat the underlying causes of colds. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate cold symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing congestion and promoting overall respiratory health.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: Exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with colds.
Saponins: Display antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help combat respiratory infections and reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of colds and flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for colds because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions for colds because they contain saponins and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat infections.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make decoctions for colds because they contain mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a large amount of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves for decoction preparation.
Measure one tablespoon of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves per cup of water.
Combine the measured dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves with 8 ounces of water.
Boil the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes over low heat to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
